title: Governance Runtime Failure Mode Test Matrix
purpose: Define the first machine-readable break-test scenarios for runtime trust validation.

scenarios.description: A required evidence input declared by policy or domain contract is absent at verdict time.
scenarios.fault_injection: Omit a required evidence payload while keeping policy scope unchanged.
scenarios.expected_runtime_behavior: Emit a constrained reviewable verdict and request the missing evidence instead of silently passing.
scenarios.determinism_assertion: Given the same missing-evidence snapshot and policy snapshot, repeated evaluation must produce the same verdict impact.

scenarios.description: An evidence payload exists but does not conform to the declared schema or provenance contract.
scenarios.fault_injection: Provide an evidence payload with schema mismatch or invalid envelope metadata.
scenarios.expected_runtime_behavior: Reject the invalid evidence as non-conforming and fail closed for the affected decision.
scenarios.determinism_assertion: Given the same invalid payload and policy snapshot, repeated evaluation must preserve the same violation classification and verdict.

scenarios.description: Two policies claim precedence over the same decision boundary in a way the runtime must resolve.
scenarios.fault_injection: Inject a domain-level or repo-local override that conflicts with runtime safety policy.
scenarios.expected_runtime_behavior: Resolve according to precedence rules or escalate with trace when automatic resolution is not legal.
scenarios.determinism_assertion: The same policy snapshot conflict must not alternate between pass and stop across repeated runs.

scenarios.description: The runtime or an adapter fails during decision computation or artifact emission.
scenarios.fault_injection: Force a runtime exception during violation classification or artifact generation.
scenarios.expected_runtime_behavior: Fail closed for protected domains or emit an explicit escalation record instead of returning an implicit success.
scenarios.determinism_assertion: Equivalent runtime failures under the same runtime version and input snapshot must not downgrade into success on replay.

scenarios.description: The same evidence snapshot and policy snapshot are replayed to verify stable verdict output.
scenarios.fault_injection: Replay the identical evidence, policy, and runtime version inputs across repeated evaluations.
scenarios.expected_runtime_behavior: Produce the same verdict, preserve the same cited evidence and policy snapshots, and allow only trace-shape variation within declared limits.
scenarios.determinism_assertion: The same input snapshot must produce the same verdict; only declared trace variation is allowed.
